title: Pholidia santalina
taxonomy: Pholidia santalina is a species within the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the class Magnoliopsida and the division Magnoliophyta. This plant is further classified into the order Sapindales and the order Sapindales is a part of the clade Rosidae. The kingdom to which Pholidia santalina belongs is Plantae. The rank below genus in its classification is species.
uses: Pholidia santalina has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, including its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health. Some sources suggest that the plant may have antibacterial and antifungal properties. It may also be used to treat skin conditions, such as wounds and infections. Further studies are necessary to confirm the plant's efficacy and potential side effects.
benefits: Pholidia santalina is known for its potential health benefits, including its ability to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis. properties: Pholidia santalina, also known as the Santalum tree, is believed to possess several therapeutic properties. The plant's leaves and bark contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial in treating various health conditions. constituents: Pholidia santalina contains active constituents such as santalin, which is a mixture of isosantalenone and santalic acid. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Santalin has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin problems and wounds. The active constituents in Pholidia santalina may also exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. parts: Pholidia santalina is not a known plant.
